title: centaurea stenolepis subsp. bosniaca
intro: Centaurea stenolepis subsp. bosniaca, also known as the Bosnian knapweed or mountain knapweed, is a subspecies of the Centaurea stenolepis plant, which belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea stenolepis subsp. bosniaca include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine in the Balkan region.
